question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
narrative_context: Pentagon readying 'for weeks of US ground operations' but explicitly short of full invasion (Al Jazeera, March 29 2026)
Pentagon official: full-scale ground invasion requires Congressional declaration + 6-12 month mobilization — not feasible by EOY (Washington Post, March 28 2026)
NATO intelligence consensus: ground invasion would require 200K+ troops and allied support; Europe refusing military participation (leaked, March 26 2026)
No heavy armored units, logistics depth, or command structures for prolonged land war currently deployed — force is tactical/selective not strategic (Military.com analysis, March 30 2026)
Market conflating ongoing air campaign (Operation Epic Fury, started Feb 28) with full invasion — semantically distinct outcomes
thesis: Market is pricing full invasion at ~95%+ probability, but Pentagon, State Dept, and NATO all confirm operations are weeks-long raids on coastal sites (Kharg Island, Strait of Hormuz), not invasion. Full invasion requires Congressional declaration, 6-12 months mobilization, 200K+ troops, and allied support — none of which are happening. Historical base rate: Iraq 2003 took 18 months of buildup. Market is conflating air/cyber campaign with invasion. Real probability: 4%.
x_thread: Market says 95%+ chance US invades Iran by EOY. But Pentagon is explicitly prepping for 'weeks' of limited raids—not invasion. There's a semantic difference. 🧵
Full invasion requires: Congressional declaration, 6-12 month mobilization, 200K+ troops, allied support. Pentagon says NONE of this is happening. (WaPo, March 28)
NATO intel leaked: Europe refusing military participation. No heavy armor, logistics, or command structures for prolonged land war. This is raid force, not invasion force.
Market is conflating Operation Epic Fury (air/cyber since Feb 28) with invasion. Check our signal for the real probability: https://dub.sh/pb-x
